flashcards.definition: P₁/T₁ = P₂/T₂; at constant volume, pressure is directly proportional to temperature (K).
The container's volume doesn't change (e.g., a rigid sealed container).
Heating a gas in a rigid container increases pressure (more energetic collisions).
Pressure cookers, tire pressure increasing on hot days.
Aerosol cans can explode if heated — Gay-Lussac's Law in action.
